title: eriogonum umbellatum subsp. aridum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum subsp. aridum, commonly known as Mormon tea, is a subspecies of the buckwheat sedge that belongs to the Polygonaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Mormon tea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
